title: Ehrharta filiformis
intro: Ehrharta filiformis, also known as Slender Veldtgrass, is a perennial grass native to South Africa and is often used in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Ehrharta filiformis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Ehrharta filiformis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Ehrharta filiformis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory infections and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Ehrharta filiformis is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. This family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Ehrharta filiformis being a part of the subfamily Danthonioideae. Within the subfamily, it belongs to the tribe Danthonieae. The exact classification of Ehrharta filiformis may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a member of the family Poaceae. The species is also known by the common names fine-leaved Ehrharta or fine-leaved button-grass.
